Title: The GmPRL1b-GmST2-GmAOC3/4 Module Confers Salt Tolerance and Botrytis cinerea Resistance by Inducing Jasmonic Acid Biosynthesis in Soybean

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript induced by NaCl at 6 h in roots; downregulated by H2O2 at 2, 6 and 12 h and by MeJA at 0.5 and 6 h; protein level showed no significant response to salt or Botrytis cinerea in constitutive hairy-root expression system
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upstream stress-responsive regulator in soybean roots; overexpression in hairy roots elevates downstream JA-pathway gene expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmPRL1b physically interacts with GmST2 in the nucleus, confirmed by Y2H, BiFC, and Co-IP. 2. GmPRL1b increases GmST2 protein accumulation through post-translational regulation without changing GmST2 transcript abundance. 3. GmPRL1b overexpression upregulates GmAOC3, GmAOC4, and GmPR1 and increases JA accumulation, thereby enhancing salt tolerance and Botrytis cinerea resistance. 4. GmST2 knockdown suppresses the effect of GmPRL1b, indicating GmPRL1b functions upstream of GmST2.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl; Botrytis cinerea infection; H2O2; methyl jasmonate (MeJA)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid accumulation and enhances salt tolerance and Botrytis cinerea resistance by stabilizing GmST2 upstream of JA biosynthesis genes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-immunoprecipitation; transient overexpression and RNAi in soybean hairy roots; immunoblotting for GmST2 abundance; JA quantification; RT-qPCR; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant tolerance to salinity stress and resistance to Botrytis cinerea; mediates cross-protection between abiotic and biotic stress
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to elevate JA-mediated dual stress resistance, but best deployed together with functional GmST2 because its effect depends on GmST2 activity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty reported; study emphasizes improved stress resistance without apparent inhibition of normal growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a Common Gene rather than pathway enzyme or TF; proposed module forms a positive feedback loop with JA and GmST2. Inhibitors MG132, E-64, and BFA did not increase GmST2 accumulation, suggesting regulation is not via tested proteasome/cysteine protease/autophagy-related routes

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway; allene oxide cyclization step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key JA-biosynthesis enzyme targeted by the core regulator, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct biosynthetic intermediate precursor of jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript strongly upregulated in GmST2-overexpressing soybean roots and downregulated in gmst2 gmst2h mutant leaves; directly activated by GmST2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAOC3 is one of the most highly upregulated JA-biosynthesis genes in GmST2-overexpressing soybean. 2. GmST2 directly binds the GmAOC3 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. GmST2 activates GmAOC3 promoter activity through specific NAC-binding motifs. 4. Upregulation of GmAOC3 is associated with elevated jasmonic acid accumulation and improved salt tolerance/Botrytis resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl; Botrytis cinerea infection; JA-pathway inhibitor DIECA; exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GmAOC3 expression promotes JA biosynthesis contributing to enhanced salt tolerance and Botrytis cinerea resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase assay; JA quantification in genetic backgrounds altering upstream regulators
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-defense hormone mediating salinity tolerance and fungal resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target or stack partner with GmST2/GmPRL1b to boost JA-mediated stress resilience
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in binding/activation assays; 35S promoter used for upstream regulator constructs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains NAC core motifs bound by GmST2; selected as one of two direct target AOC genes for mechanistic validation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway; allene oxide cyclization step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key JA-biosynthesis enzyme; prior soybean evidence cited for JA increase, but rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct biosynthetic intermediate precursor of jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in GmST2-overexpressing roots and downregulated in gmst2 gmst2h leaves; direct transcriptional target of GmST2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAOC4 is strongly induced in GmST2-overexpressing soybean and reduced in gmst2 gmst2h mutants. 2. GmST2 directly binds the GmAOC4 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. GmST2 activates GmAOC4 transcription via specific NAC-binding motifs. 4. GmAOC4 activation contributes to increased JA accumulation underlying salt tolerance and Botrytis resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl; Botrytis cinerea infection; DIECA; exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased GmAOC4 expression promotes JA accumulation and thereby supports salt tolerance and fungal resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase assay; metabolite profiling of JA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates abiotic and biotic stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for gene stacking with GmST2/GmPRL1b to enhance JA-mediated cross-stress protection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in binding/activation assays; 35S promoter used for upstream regulator constructs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported to increase JA levels in soybean and was the most highly upregulated validated target in this study

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots, leaves, and flowers; moderate in stems and seeds; rapidly induced by NaCl and Botrytis cinerea, peaking at 6 h and 0.5 h respectively; also induced by JA and H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in soybean elevates JA-biosynthesis gene expression and JA accumulation; protein abundance continues increasing during 24 h of NaCl or Botrytis treatment
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: NaCl; Botrytis cinerea; jasmonic acid; H2O2; GmPRL1b-mediated protein stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of JA-biosynthesis genes; positive feedback with JA; post-translational stabilization by GmPRL1b
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmAOC3 and GmAOC4 directly; upregulates broader JA biosynthesis genes including LOX, AOS, AOC, and OPR homologs; promotes GmPR1 expression indirectly through increased JA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonic acid biosynthesis and downstream JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop: JA induces GmST2 expression, while GmST2 promotes JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with GmPRL1b in the nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid accumulation, salt tolerance, Botrytis cinerea resistance, and plant growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmST2 is a salt- and Botrytis-induced NAC transcriptional activator localized in the nucleus. 2. Overexpression enhances growth, salt tolerance, and Botrytis resistance, whereas gmst2 gmst2h mutants show opposite phenotypes. 3. GmST2 upregulates JA-biosynthesis genes and directly binds/activates GmAOC3 and GmAOC4 promoters, increasing JA accumulation. 4. JA is required for GmST2-mediated salt tolerance, as DIECA abolishes the phenotype and exogenous JA rescues the mutant. 5. GmPRL1b acts upstream by stabilizing GmST2 protein. 6. Natural variation in GmST2 associates with better growth and yield in saline fields.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 double mutant; RNAi hairy roots; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana tabacum; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; JA quantification; inhibitor and exogenous JA complementation assays; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates salinity tolerance and resistance to Botrytis cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl; Botrytis cinerea infection; jasmonic acid; H2O2; DIECA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive activator of JA accumulation under salt and fungal stress through direct induction of key JA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent breeding target for stress-resilient soybean because elite haplotypes improve plant height and yield in saline fields without evident growth penalty; suitable for marker-assisted selection, allele mining, or transgenic/editing approaches
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent growth penalty; instead promotes growth under non-stressed and stressed conditions, though authors note JA pathway is likely not the sole mechanism controlling growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected during domestication; Hap1/HapA elite alleles show stronger transactivation and superior agronomic performance in saline fields. GmST2h was used with GmST2 in double knockout due to redundancy, but mechanistic focus centered on GmST2
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native promoter assayed for haplotype activity

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern highly correlated with GmST2 under salt and drought stress among ATAF1 orthologs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied only in combination with GmST2 in double knockout mutant
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salt and drought-associated expression pattern correlated with GmST2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely redundant transcriptional regulation with GmST2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Likely contributes to jasmonic acid-related stress responses through functional redundancy with GmST2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss together with GmST2 reduces salt tolerance, Botrytis cinerea resistance, growth, and JA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmST2h is the closest soybean paralog of GmST2 and shows correlated stress-induced expression, suggesting redundancy. 2. CRISPR-Cas9 double knockout of GmST2 and GmST2h causes increased salt sensitivity, reduced growth, larger Botrytis lesions, and reduced JA-associated gene expression/JA content. 3. The paper does not separately dissect direct molecular targets of GmST2h.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; expression profiling; CRISPR-Cas9 double knockout with GmST2; stress phenotyping; RT-qPCR; JA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent redundant contributor inferred from double-mutant phenotypes, but not mechanistically established alone
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential redundant backup locus, but direct utility is less established than GmST2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because it was directly edited and phenotypically tested in the Results, although mechanistic focus remains on GmST2
